answer:
Manu introduces the eight forms of marriage at the start of Manusmṛti chapter 3 (verses 20–21), explicitly naming them **in order** as: Brāhma, Daiva, Ārṣa, Prājāpatya, Āsura, Gāndharva, Rākṣasa, and Paiśāca.[^1]

He then describes each **in the same order** across the following verses (27–34):[^2]

- **Brāhma** (3.27): Gift of a daughter, after decking her and honoring her, to a man learned in the Veda and of good conduct, whom the father himself invites.[^3]
- **Daiva** (3.28): Gift of a daughter, after having adorned her, to a priest who is duly officiating at a sacrifice while it is in progress.[^4]
- **Ārṣa** (3.29): Father gives away his daughter according to the rule, after receiving from the bridegroom a pair of cattle for the fulfillment of the sacred law.[^5]
- **Prājāpatya** (3.30): Gift of a daughter after honoring the couple and addressing them with the words, "May you both perform your Dharma together."[^6]
- **Āsura** (3.31): Bridegroom receives a maiden after having given wealth (by his own will and as much as he is able) to her kinsmen and to the bride herself.[^7]
- **Gāndharva** (3.32): Voluntary union of a maiden and her lover, which springs from desire and has sexual union as its purpose.[^8]
- **Rākṣasa** (3.33): Forcible abduction of a maiden from her home, while she cries out and weeps, after her kinsmen have been slain, wounded, and their houses broken into.[^9]
- **Paiśāca** (3.34): Man secretly approaches a girl who is sleeping, intoxicated, or mentally disordered.[^10]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-b07c61).
[^2]: Manusmṛti, [verses 27–34](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-93ddb7).
[^3]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-93ddb7).
[^4]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-7c3179).
[^5]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-831e2c).
[^6]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-c50f0a).
[^7]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-4b354a).
[^8]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-beb0f2).
[^9]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-2f28cf).
[^10]: Manusmṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-ca03b2).
